content: If we integrate the 2025 iteration of the ICM curiosity mechanism as a dynamic reward re-weighting module for long-horizon autonomous agent planning algorithms, we can reframe environmental exploration as a built-in planner that automatically generates subgoals instead of relying on manually designed pathfinding steps. This turns static, hand-specified planning objectives get replaced by intrinsic curiosity-driven exploration that adapts the agent’s search tree expansion based on prediction error novelty, resulting in more robust open-ended long-horizon plans in partially observable environments.
